posts.title: First Autonomous Navigation Test: Surprises, Fixes, and Lessons Learned
posts.excerpt: After three weeks of ground testing, simulation validation, and parameter refinement, the Raptor-VII took to the skies for its first fully autonomous mission on February 14th at Meridian Airfield. The aircraft completed the planned waypoint circuit successfully, but not without surprises — a 3.2 m cross-track error in the third leg revealed a compass interference issue from the new power distribution board. We share what happened, what we changed, and what the second flight revealed.
posts.content:
After three weeks of ground testing, simulation validation, and parameter refinement, the Raptor-VII took to the skies for its first fully autonomous mission on February 14th at Meridian Airfield.

The aircraft completed the planned waypoint circuit successfully, but not without surprises — a 3.2 m cross-track error in the third leg revealed a compass interference issue from the new power distribution board. We share what happened, what we changed, and what the second flight revealed.

What We Found

The compass deviation was traced to a high-current path on the PDB running within 4 cm of the external compass module. Rerouting the battery leads and adding ferrite beads reduced the deviation from 8° to under 0.5°.

Lessons Learned

  • Always perform a full compass calibration after any PDB change
  • HIL simulation cannot reproduce EMI effects — field testing is essential
  • The ArduPilot EKF3 compass weight parameter needs tuning per vehicle

posts.title: Materials Matter: Why We Switched to Carbon Fiber Composite for the Raptor-VII
posts.excerpt: Previous seasons used a foam/fibreglass hybrid that met weight targets but showed fatigue cracking after heavy landing cycles. After a full trade study comparing six construction approaches, we chose a 3-layer CF/Kevlar monocoque. This video walks through the complete layup process, vacuum bagging procedure, and the structural test results that confirmed a 2.4 kg empty weight with a 10 G ultimate load factor.
posts.content:
Previous seasons used a foam/fibreglass hybrid that met weight targets but showed fatigue cracking after heavy landing cycles. After a full trade study comparing six construction approaches, we chose a 3-layer CF/Kevlar monocoque.

This post walks through the complete layup process and the structural test results that confirmed a 2.4 kg empty weight with a 10 G ultimate load factor.

posts.title: ODLC Pipeline v2.0: Achieving 94% Target Classification Accuracy
posts.mediaAlt: ODLC detection output showing classified targets
posts.excerpt: After the first flight test exposed false positives from road markings and fence shadows, we retrained our YOLOv8 model with 1,200 additional synthetic samples and real flight images. We also added a secondary classifier for letter recognition using PaddleOCR. The results: 94.2% correct end-to-end classifications across 350 validation images taken from 40 m AGL. Full benchmark methodology and confusion matrix inside.
posts.content:
After the first flight test exposed false positives from road markings and fence shadows, we retrained our YOLOv8 model with 1,200 additional synthetic samples and real flight images.

The results: 94.2% correct end-to-end classifications across 350 validation images taken from 40 m AGL.

posts.title: Integration Week: When 6 Subsystems Become One Aircraft
posts.mediaAlt: Team members conducting systems integration testing in the lab
posts.excerpt: Integration week is the moment of truth for every competition team — when software written in isolation meets hardware designed by a different person. We document the five biggest issues discovered during I4A's 2026 integration sprint: a PWM timing conflict between the autopilot and the payload servo, an EMI source from the ESC affecting GPS signal quality, and three others — plus every fix we applied.
posts.content:
Integration week is the moment of truth for every competition team — when software written in isolation meets hardware designed by a different person.

We document the five biggest issues discovered during I4A's 2026 integration sprint and every fix we applied.

posts.title: Competition Ready: Our SUAS 2026 Mission Strategy and Task Prioritization
posts.mediaAlt: I4A team in discussion planning competition strategy
posts.excerpt: With six weeks to go, the Raptor-VII has completed 24 autonomous flights totalling 19.4 flight hours. We've locked the airframe configuration and moved into full mission rehearsals. This post lays out our task scoring strategy — how we decided which SUAS tasks to prioritize, what our rehearsal schedule looks like, and how we're preparing the team mentally and logistically for competition week.
posts.content: With six weeks to go, the Raptor-VII has completed 24 autonomous flights totalling 19.4 flight hours. We've locked the airframe configuration and moved into full mission rehearsals.

posts.title: Introducing the I4A Raptor-VII: Our 2026 Competition Platform
posts.mediaAlt: Industry 4.0 Aerospace logo announcing the Raptor-VII
posts.excerpt: After six months of design work, structural analysis, and system architecture planning, we're proud to unveil the I4A Raptor-VII — our seventh-generation competition aircraft. This post covers the design philosophy behind the Raptor-VII, the key lessons from the Raptor-VI season that shaped our decisions, and the technical targets we've set for the 2026 SUAS competition.
posts.content: After six months of design work, structural analysis, and system architecture planning, we're proud to unveil the I4A Raptor-VII — our seventh-generation competition aircraft.
